quality.rationale: 经典引力波条形探测器模态分析，具备实际物理背景与阻尼谐振机制，但解答文本截断严重缺失关键步骤

origin.question: Problem 18.12. An elastic rod can be used to detect gravitational waves not only at its lowest normal mode frequency $\omega_0$ , but also at harmonics$\omega_{n} \equiv n \omega_{0}$ . What is the sensitivity of the $n^{\text{th}}$ mode relative to the zeroth, i.e. how does the ratio of maximum squared amplitude of the displacement to energy flux of wave vary with $n$ ? (Assume the rod has the same mechanical damping time for all modes.)
origin.solution: Solution 18.12. The normal modes of the rod (taken to be oriented along the $x$ -axis) are found from the equation$$\frac {\partial^{2} \xi}{\partial t^{2}} + \frac {1}{r} \frac {\partial \xi}{\partial t} - a^{2} \frac {\partial^{2} \xi}{\partial x^{2}} = 0. \tag {1}$$Here $\xi(\mathbf{x}, t)$ is the displacement of an element of the rod, $\tau$ is the damping time and $a$ is the speed of sound. The modes are of the form$$\xi = e^{- i \omega t} e^{- t / 2 \tau} u (x) \tag {2}$$where$$a^{2} \frac {d^{2} u}{d x^{2}} + \left(\omega^{2} + \frac {1}{4 r^{2}}\right) u = 0. \tag {3}$$
